summary: Post-transplantation, Cytomegalovirus (CMV) remains a significant concern requiring vigilant monitoring and management as part of comprehensive care strategies after transplant surgery. Key preventive measures include targeted antiviral therapy such as ganciclovir or valganciclovir to mitigate risks associated with this prevalent infectious threat among transplanted patients. Agent2 identified CMV as the predominant post-transplant infection based on its recurrent focus throughout medical literature regarding immunosuppressed states typical immediately following an organ transplant procedure.
